commit_scope: Add a focused no-HTTP/no-Ollama validation lane showing a Form-emitted Metal inference kernel loading model weight bytes at runtime. This closes a small boundary only: byte-loaded weights into a standalone Metal runtime artifact, not full GGUF parsing, full llama generation, or a direct fkwu Metal primitive.

results: metal-emit-band verdict 31; fourth arm: 1 band(s) four-way (fkwu + pre-flattened tables); 1 ok, 0 divergent

standalone runtime artifact passed under env -i with empty PATH; denied toolchain names visible on PATH = 0; http_or_ollama=absent; model_bytes_loaded=44; weight_bytes_loaded=24; input_bytes_loaded=12; gpu_y=[-0.250000,5.000000]; expected_y=[-0.250000,5.000000]; max_delta=0.000000000; verdict=PASS form-native-metal-weight-bytes-runtime

ci_validation.reason: No CI run was opened for this local proof-lane patch.

deploy_validation.reason: No deploy surface changed; this is a local Metal runtime-boundary proof.

phase_gate.reason: Local proof is complete for the small boundary; CI/deploy were not run.

observed_existing_surfaces.fkwu_direct_metal_primitive: not found; fkwu_bridge.go states fkwu makes no host call in that offload path
observed_existing_surfaces.fkwu_input_bytes: present as argv[3] staged into fk_src for programs that read fk-buf; this is an input channel, not Metal execution
observed_existing_surfaces.kernel_file_byte_load: read_file_bytes is present in sibling kernels and recipes such as q4k-dequant use real llama weight bytes as Form data
observed_existing_surfaces.metal_emission: jte-matvec-msl emits deterministic MSL and metal-emit-band proves it four-way
observed_existing_surfaces.metal_runtime: existing scripts prove Form-emitted kernels through Swift/Metal host carriers; GPU_GAPS names weight load to device as not yet wired

build_phase.model_fixture_layout: u32 rows, u32 cols, f32 weights, f32 input

runtime_phase.artifact_shape: single compiled Swift executable with Form-emitted Metal source embedded; model weights supplied as a separate binary file

remaining_boundaries: There is still no direct fkwu primitive that invokes Metal.
The build phase still uses shell, form-kernel-go, and xcrun swiftc.
The runtime artifact uses Metal.framework makeLibrary(source:) over embedded MSL; it is not direct AIR/metallib/AGX bytes.
The model fixture is a small matvec byte layout; it is not full GGUF parsing, llama tokenizer, KV-cache decode, or multi-layer generation.
Weight bytes are loaded into shared Metal buffers for one dispatch; persistent resident model weights and full scheduler integration remain open.
